rationale: The US-Iran conflict continues with Trump explicitly threatening to seize Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal — a qualitative escalation in rhetoric that keeps energy price risk for Poland elevated, while the World Bank warning of a post-COVID economic low adds indirect economic pressure; no new direct threat to Poland itself.

top_events.title: Trump threatens to seize Iran's Kharg Island; US-Iran strikes continue
top_events.summary: Trump explicitly stated the US will 'be taking' Kharg Island — Iran's main oil export hub — while mutual US-Iran strikes continue for a second day. If Kharg Island is struck or seized, global oil supply would be severely disrupted, directly hitting Polish fuel and energy prices.

top_events.title: World Bank: US-Iran war to drag global economy to post-COVID low
top_events.summary: The World Bank warns the ongoing US-Iran conflict will pull the global economy to its weakest point since COVID-19. This poses an indirect but real risk to Poland's export-driven economy, employment, and financial stability.

top_events.title: Gold overtakes dollar as top reserve asset
top_events.summary: Gold has surpassed the US dollar as the world's top reserve asset, signalling growing doubts about dollar dominance. This reflects broader financial instability that could affect Polish złoty, interest rates, and import costs indirectly.
